div.h2: Where does the <1% cost come from?

g.text: Signal delivery | 1× | ~100 ns | One timer fire
Flag check | 10,000× | ~1 ns each | ~10 μs total
Total overhead | — | ~10.15 μs | Per time slice

div.p: Key Insight: The flag check is a single atomic load that's almost always in L1 cache and branch-predicted as "not taken." The CPU speculatively continues executing useful work. The actual preemption (context switch) happens only once per time slice, so the 50ns cost is amortized over 10ms of work. Compare to OS preemption: ~1-2 μs per switch, 100× more expensive than our coroutine switch.
